topic: Marin Daily: Component sourcing, optical stack, electrical design, firmware progress — Friday Mar 6
summary: Mobile optical ships with App Store submission while component sourcing research runs in parallel.

actionItems.text: Complete component sourcing supplier validation research
actionItems.description: Research and validate precision optics suppliers for future hardware expansion. This involves identifying suppliers with 12-16 week lead times, getting prototype component quotes, and establishing supply chain relationships without impacting mobile development timeline. Creates optionality for hardware roadmap while keeping mobile development on track.
acceptanceCriteria: List of 3-5 validated precision optics suppliers with contact information
Prototype component sourcing quotes for key optical elements
Timeline documentation showing no impact on mobile optical delivery

actionItems.description: Create technical documentation showing how optical and biometric data processing stays local on device with no cloud transmission. This addresses ethical concerns around faith content privacy while enabling optical feature development. Documentation supports both development implementation and customer transparency requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Technical architecture document showing local-only data processing
Privacy policy language for optical features

actionItems.text: Provide optical feature specifications for marketing materials
actionItems.description: Create customer-facing technical specifications for mobile optical features focusing on capabilities and user benefits rather than implementation details. This supports App Store submission copy and marketing positioning for strong launch. Specifications should highlight value proposition while maintaining technical accuracy.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature specification document with user benefits focus
Technical capabilities summary for marketing use
Integration guidelines for App Store submission copy

actionItems.text: Make final go/no-go decision on App Store submission timeline
actionItems.description: Decide whether to include optical features in initial App Store submission versus day-one update based on review time versus launch positioning tradeoffs. This decision impacts marketing timeline, development priorities, and revenue validation strategy. Must balance speed to market with competitive positioning strength.
acceptanceCriteria: Timeline impact analysis for both options

topPriorities: Ship mobile optical with current App Store build
Complete component sourcing supplier validation research
Implement privacy-first optical architecture with local processing

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Keep faith content as separate standalone section (not merged into Family tab)
Keep cash liquid — no deployment until post-launch revenue data
App Store submission: full current build (all 4 tabs) as-is with faith lessons. Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update.
Paper trading MVP: manual entry (6 weeks), basic free / advanced analytics premium. Integrate into current Lever app finance tab.
Launch with delayed market data for both free and premium tiers. Real-time as future ultra-premium.
Org flattened to 8 C-suite agents. No permanent sub-agents — spawn dynamically via sessions_spawn.

typeName: Marin Daily: Component sourcing, optical stack, electrical design, firmware progress
